steps.title: Find sin w from the Pythagorean identity
steps.content:
Given $\cos w° = \frac{9}{41}$, use $\sin^2 w° + \cos^2 w° = 1$ to find $\sin w°$:

$$\sin^2 w° = 1 - \left(\frac{9}{41}\right)^2 = 1 - \frac{81}{1681} = \frac{1600}{1681}$$

$$\sin w° = \frac{40}{41}$$

(This is the familiar 9-40-41 Pythagorean triple.)

steps.title: Recognize the altitude-to-hypotenuse configuration
steps.content:
The figure shows right triangle $ABC$ with a right angle at $A$ and $D$ on the hypotenuse $BC$.

The right angle marked at $D$ indicates that $AD \perp BC$ — this is the altitude from $A$ to the hypotenuse.

In this classic configuration, similar triangles reveal that $\angle ABD = \angle CAD$.

Since $\angle ABD = w°$, we have $\angle CAD = w°$, so $z = w$.

steps.title: Calculate tan z using the angle equality
steps.content:
Since $z = w$:

$$\tan z° = \tan w° = \frac{\sin w°}{\cos w°} = \frac{\frac{40}{41}}{\frac{9}{41}} = \frac{40}{41} \cdot \frac{41}{9} = \frac{40}{9}$$

Equivalent forms accepted: 40/9, 4.444.
